problem_summary: 2015 MCM Problem B (discrete): design a search plan for a missing transoceanic plane that has left radar coverage (>200 mi from coast), with scarce prior information (motivated by MH370 and AF447). The team builds an end-to-end probabilistic Search-and-Rescue (SAR) pipeline: (1) a probability model for WHERE the plane impacted the water, (2) a detection model for how likely a search aircraft spots it per pass, and (3) an adaptive search-ordering algorithm that updates region probabilities as cells are cleared and decides which cell to fly to next. Demonstrated on a fictional Boston→Lisbon Boeing 777 flight (Flight ABCD) with concrete latitude/longitude outputs.
models_used: Probability of Containment (POC) model: a custom quadratic, concave-down PDF f_Yc(y_c) = 3/(y_B-y_R)^3 * (y_C-(y_B-y_R))^2 for distance-to-casualty along the intended flight path (IFP), encoding that loss-of-contact most likely means failure occurred near the last contact point (deliberately rejecting the standard uniform-time assumption)

Glide/ballistic trajectory model: impact distance Y_I = Y_C + G*h, where G is the aircraft glide ratio (~19:1 for a 777) and h is cruising altitude; radar horizon y_R = sqrt(200^2 - h^2) derived via Pythagorean theorem
Change-of-variables (transformation technique) to derive the PDF of Y_I analytically from the PDF of Y_C

Off-course (veering) model: a Normal/Gaussian distribution perpendicular to the IFP with sigma_x = G*h*sin(pi/8), capping plausible veer at pi/8 (22.5 deg); combined with the along-track PDF as a joint density f(x,y_I)=f(x)*f(y_I) assuming independence

Discretization into an N x 6 grid of search cells, with N = ceil((y_B - y_R - G*h)/(t_0.5 * v_s)) chosen so each cell takes ~0.5 hr to sweep; POC per cell computed by double-integrating the joint density (Mathematica loop), exploiting symmetry about the IFP

Coordinate transform from an IFP-referenced (x,y) frame to latitude/longitude via a 2x2 rotation matrix R (angle phi = arctan(r_e/r_n)), scaling by miles-per-degree rho, and translation: C_L = rho*R*C + y_0 (validated by det(R)=1)

Probability of Detection (POD) model: Koopman's exponential random-search law POD = 1 - e^(-coverage), with effective sweep width S = 2*sqrt((kappa*epsilon)^2 - h_s^2) (sensor range epsilon, search altitude h_s, weather scalar kappa in [0,1]), effort = d_s*n for n search planes

Bayesian-style POC-updating rule: after searching cell q without success, POC_q' = POC_q*(1-POD_q), and the lost mass (POC_q - POC_q') is redistributed uniformly as (POC_q - POC_q')/(6N-1) across the other cells (conservation of total probability)

Greedy neighborhood (nearest-adjacent) search-ordering algorithm: after updating the map, pick the highest-POC cell among the 3x3 adjacent neighborhood as the next target, minimizing non-adjacent flight to save fuel/time; compared against a non-adjacency-restricted greedy baseline to show reduced back-and-forth travel
key_techniques: Decomposing the problem cleanly into the canonical SAR triad POC -> POD -> updating, which maps the open-ended prompt onto established search-theory structure (cited Stone 1977, Frost 1999, Koopman)
Building a bespoke PDF from a physical narrative (failure-soon-after-contact) rather than reaching for a default distribution, then justifying its concave-down quadratic shape
Analytic derivation chain: casualty PDF -> glide transform -> impact PDF -> joint 2D density, all in closed form, then numerically integrated over the grid
Tying every parameter to a real, citable physical quantity: 200-mi radar radius, 19:1 glide ratio of a 777, 40,000 ft cruise altitude, search-plane speed/altitude/sensor range
Embedding a weather/condition fudge factor kappa in [0,1] inside Koopman's law to make POD tunable without abandoning a standard model
Probability conservation in the update step (mass removed from a cleared cell is re-added elsewhere), giving a self-consistent dynamic POC map
Using a controlled comparison (adjacency-constrained vs. unconstrained greedy search, Appendices C vs D) to empirically justify the neighborhood restriction
Full reproducibility: Mathematica code for integration, POC matrix, and both search loops, plus Excel tables for the rotation/coordinate math, all in appendices
A concrete worked example (Flight ABCD, Boston->Lisbon, 777) that carries plugged-in numbers all the way to four bounding lat/long coordinates of an actual search rectangle
why_it_won: From a judge's perspective this paper wins because it is a complete, self-consistent, and operationally actionable pipeline rather than a collection of formulas. It (1) grounds an abstract probabilistic model in real aviation physics (glide ratio, radar horizon, cruise altitude) so the math is never arbitrary; (2) makes a defensible, non-obvious modeling choice (quadratic concave-down casualty PDF) and explicitly argues why it beats the textbook uniform assumption used in prior SAR work; (3) closes the loop with an adaptive Bayesian-flavored update and a resource-aware greedy search order, directly answering the 'what do searchers actually DO' question; (4) produces a tangible deliverable - real latitude/longitude search-cell corners - so a rescue team could literally fly the plan; (5) demonstrates engineering judgment by comparing its neighborhood-constrained search against an unconstrained baseline and quantitatively defending the restriction; and (6) is fully reproducible with Mathematica/Excel appendices. It is honest about sensitivity and weaknesses, signaling mature modeling. The framing around MH370/AF447 gives it timeliness and motivation that judges reward.
section_highlights: Structure mirrors the model's logic and is easy for a judge to follow: a strong abstract that states the POC->POD->update arc in plain language; a layered Model section that derives the casualty PDF, then the glide transform, then the perpendicular Normal, then the joint density, then discretization, then the lat/long coordinate transform, then POD, then the updating algorithm. Each component is introduced with a one-line physical motivation before the math. Figures do real work: Fig 1 defines sigma_x geometrically, Fig 2 shows the N x 6 grid, Fig 9 the joint density surface, Fig 11 the actual POC heat-map matrix, and Figs 12 vs 13 visually contrast the adjacency-constrained vs unconstrained search paths. A dedicated Assumptions list (9 explicit assumptions) and a multi-parameter Sensitivity Analysis (f(G), f(theta), POD vs theta/G/h_s/n) precede an honest Strengths/Weaknesses section. The standout writing move is the separate plain-English 'Summary for an Airline's Press Conference' that re-explains the whole method without equations for a lay/stakeholder audience - directly satisfying the problem's communication deliverable.
pitfalls_or_limitations: No ocean currents / drift modeling - debris and the plane are assumed to stay exactly where they hit the water, which is physically unrealistic for a multi-day search and is the model's biggest acknowledged gap

Rigid geometric assumptions: IFP is a straight A->B line, plane does not explode in air (intact glide only), constant velocities for both lost and search planes, plane stays afloat/detectable for the entire search - several are fragile

Self-admitted POC mis-allocation: because sigma_x comes from a triangular (sin) construction but cells are rectangular, cells just past 1 sigma at large G*h are over-allocated and outer/later partitions are under-allocated
The pi/8 veer cap and the casualty PDF's specific quadratic form are asserted by intuition, not derived from data or validated against the real AF447/MH370 outcomes that motivate the paper

The model is explicitly NOT robust - the authors concede high sensitivity to G, theta, h_s; small parameter changes swing POC and POD substantially, and they reframe this fragility as 'flexibility' which is a partial rationalization
Uniform redistribution of lost probability mass ((POC_q-POC_q')/(6N-1)) is a simplification - a true Bayesian update would redistribute proportionally to remaining priors, not equally
POD assumed uniform across all cells (constant effort) despite cells having very different containment, so effort is not optimally allocated to high-POC regions
Appendix Mathematica/While-loop code has visible bracket/transcription errors and the two search-order outputs are not fully reconciled, so reproducibility is imperfect in practice
Validation is a single synthetic example (Flight ABCD) with no comparison to a known real-world recovery, so accuracy is unverified
reusable_patterns: The POC -> POD -> Bayesian-update -> search-order pipeline is a transferable template for ANY search/coverage/allocation problem (lost objects, sensor placement, inspection routing)
Derive a custom PDF from a physical story and justify it against the naive default - a repeatable way to score 'modeling insight' points instead of defaulting to uniform/normal without comment
Use change-of-variables to propagate one random variable's distribution through a deterministic physical transform (here glide) into the quantity you actually need
Combine independent 1D distributions into a joint 2D density by multiplication, then discretize via double integration over a grid - a clean recipe for spatial probability maps
Anchor every parameter to a citable real-world constant (radar radius, glide ratio, cruise altitude) so reviewers can't dismiss numbers as arbitrary
Insert a single bounded scalar (kappa in [0,1]) to graft real-world degradation (weather) onto a clean textbook model without rebuilding it
Always close the loop to an operational artifact - here, converting model-space coordinates to real lat/long via a rotation+scale+translation transform so the output is directly usable
Conserve total probability when updating a dynamic map (remove from cleared cell, redistribute the exact mass elsewhere)
Justify an algorithmic design choice empirically by running a constrained vs unconstrained variant and showing the constrained one wastes less travel (Appendices C vs D)
Write a separate equation-free stakeholder summary (the 'press conference' section) to satisfy a communication deliverable and demonstrate command of the result
Ship runnable code (Mathematica) and supporting spreadsheets in appendices to make the work reproducible and credible
